SVNSEA2E.Name: Name
SVNSEA2E.RollsExploded: The dice exploded and 10s were rerolled.
SVNSEA2E.TraitDescBrawn: Raw physical power, toughness and stamina — lifting, grappling, enduring punishment and hitting hard.
SVNSEA2E.TraitDescFinesse: Coordination, precision and speed — swordplay, marksmanship, sleight of hand and delicate work.
SVNSEA2E.TraitDescResolve: Mental and physical fortitude — willpower, composure under pressure, and shrugging off fear and pain.
SVNSEA2E.TraitDescWits: Sharpness of mind — perception, quick thinking, memory, cunning and reading a situation.
SVNSEA2E.TraitDescPanache: Charm, flair and speed of action — social grace, leadership, wit and initiative.
SVNSEA2E.TraitDescInfluence: A Villain's social reach and authority — wealth, station and the power to command others.
SVNSEA2E.TraitDescStrength: A Villain's or Brute's raw might and menace in a fight.
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescAim: Ranged attacks and marksmanship — bows, crossbows and firearms.
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescAthletics: Running, climbing, jumping, swimming and general physical feats.
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescBrawl: Unarmed and improvised fighting — fists, boots, bottles and grappling.
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescConvince: Persuasion through reason, charm or negotiation — winning hearts and changing minds.
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescEmpathy: Reading people's feelings and intentions, and understanding what drives them.
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescHide: Concealing yourself or objects — stealth, camouflage and slipping out of sight.
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescIntimidate: Bending others to your will through fear, force of personality or threat.
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescNotice: Spotting details, danger and things others miss — awareness and searching.
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescPerform: Music, acting, oratory and dance — entertaining and moving an audience.
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescRide: Handling horses and other mounts, and driving carriages.
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescSailing: Crewing and handling ships — rigging, knots, navigation and life at sea.
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescScholarship: Book learning — history, science, law, languages and research.
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescTempt: Seduction and enticement — playing on desire and vice to sway others.
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescTheft: Stealing, picking locks and pockets, and other larceny.
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescWarfare: Military knowledge — tactics, strategy, command and the tools of war.
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescWeaponry: Melee combat with weapons — swords, knives, axes and the like.
SVNSEA2E.SorceryDescHex: Hexenwerk — Eisen alchemy that brews grisly unguents to command and destroy the undead.
SVNSEA2E.SorceryDescGlamour: Glamour — the sorcery of Avalon, channeling the mantles of legendary Knights and pacts with the Sidhe.
SVNSEA2E.SorceryDescPorte: Porté — Montaigne blood sorcery that tears bleeding portals between marked people and places.
SVNSEA2E.SorceryDescSanderis: Sanderis — Sarmatian pacts with the dievas (devils), trading Corruption for elemental Deals.
SVNSEA2E.SorceryDescSorte: Sorte — the Vodacce Fate Witches' art of reading and weaving the strands of destiny (women only).
SVNSEA2E.SorceryDescDarm: Dar Matushki (Mother's Touch) — Ussuran sorcery from Matushka, granting powers tied to the land and its beasts.
SVNSEA2E.AtLeastOptions: You must choose at least {min} options.
SVNSEA2E.NoMoreThanOptions: You may choose no more than {max} options.
SVNSEA2E.ItemExists: The {type}: {name} is already assigned to the character.
SVNSEA2E.ItemDoesntExist: {name} doesn't exist in the world's items.
SVNSEA2E.WrongNation: The character's nation ({anation}) doesn't match the required background {name}'s nation ({bgnation}).
SVNSEA2E.PoolEmpty: Choose a Trait and Skill to build a dice pool.
SVNSEA2E.ToggleTheme: Switch between the Paper and Night at Sea themes
SVNSEA2E.SettingThemeHint: Choose the palette for Théah character sheets. Paper is the default aged sea-chart look; Night at Sea is the dark variant.
SVNSEA2E.PacksSeeded: Seeded {count} entries into the Théah compendia.
SVNSEA2E.CompendiumOnly: {type} items must come from a compendium — pick or drag one from the Théah compendia instead of creating a custom.
SVNSEA2E.BrowseCompendium: Browse compendium (drag an entry onto the sheet)
SVNSEA2E.WizConceptIntro: Who is your Hero? Choose a name, a Nation and a concept. Your Nation shapes your Trait bonus, starting language and which sorcery is open to you.
SVNSEA2E.WizTraitsIntro: Every Trait starts at Rank 2. Spend your 2 free points, then take your Nation Bonus (+1 to one of two Traits).
SVNSEA2E.WizBackgroundsIntro: Pick two different Backgrounds. Each grants ranks in its five Skills, its Advantages, and a Quirk. Overlapping Skills rise to Rank 2.
SVNSEA2E.WizSkillsIntro: Spend 10 Skill points on top of your Backgrounds. No Skill may go above Rank 3 at creation.
SVNSEA2E.WizAdvantagesIntro: You have 5 points to buy Advantages. Advantages from your Backgrounds are already granted for free.
SVNSEA2E.WizArcanaIntro: Choose one Virtue and one Hubris. They need not come from the same arcanum.
SVNSEA2E.WizProwessIntro: Your fighting school and your allegiances. Learn a Dueling Style if you took the Duelist Academy Advantage, and — if your story allows — join a single Secret Society.
SVNSEA2E.WizDuelStyleHint: Your Duelist Academy training taught you one Style — choose it.
SVNSEA2E.WizNoDuelistAcademy: Buy the Duelist Academy Advantage (in the Advantages step) to learn a Dueling Style.
SVNSEA2E.WizSocietyHint: Optional. A Hero may belong to only one Secret Society — click to join, click again to leave. It costs nothing but loyalty.
SVNSEA2E.WizNeedDuelStyle: Choose a Dueling Style — you purchased the Duelist Academy Advantage.
SVNSEA2E.WizNationInfoHint: The +1 applies to one of these Traits after your 2 free points. National sorcery is bought later as an Advantage.
SVNSEA2E.WizNoCompendia: The core compendia haven't loaded yet. Ask your GM to open the world once so the Backgrounds, Advantages and Arcana are seeded.
SVNSEA2E.WizPlayerOnly: The Hero Creator is only for player characters.
SVNSEA2E.WizNeedBonus: Choose which Trait gets your Nation Bonus.
SVNSEA2E.WizNeedArcana: Choose a Virtue and a Hubris.
SVNSEA2E.WizAdvBudget: That would exceed your 5 Advantage points.
SVNSEA2E.WizOpenFailed: The creator couldn't open — press F12 and check the Console for the error.
SVNSEA2E.RaisesInfo: Roll a pool of d10 equal to your Trait + Skill. Group the dice into sets that each total the threshold (usually 15) or more — every set is one Raise. Spend Raises to make things happen; leftover dice are wasted.
SVNSEA2E.TraitInfo_brawn: Raw physical power and toughness — hitting hard, grappling, lifting and shrugging off harm.
SVNSEA2E.TraitInfo_finesse: Speed, agility and precision — swordplay, marksmanship and delicate handiwork.
SVNSEA2E.TraitInfo_resolve: Willpower, grit and composure — enduring pain, fear and temptation.
SVNSEA2E.TraitInfo_wits: Intelligence and perception — reasoning, noticing details and reacting quickly.
SVNSEA2E.TraitInfo_panache: Charm, daring and flair — leadership, first impressions and dramatic style.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_aim: Ranged and thrown attacks — bows, pistols and a steady hand.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_athletics: Running, climbing, jumping, swimming and raw physical feats.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_brawl: Unarmed fighting — fists, grapples and improvised weapons.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_convince: Winning people over with reason, honesty or negotiation.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_empathy: Reading emotions and motives — and sensing when someone lies.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_hide: Stealth, concealment and blending into a crowd.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_intimidate: Bending others through fear, threats or sheer presence.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_notice: Spotting hidden details, danger and things out of place.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_perform: Music, acting, oratory and captivating an audience.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_ride: Handling horses and other mounts, even under stress.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_sailing: Crewing and steering ships — rigging, knots and navigation.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_tempt: Seduction and stirring desire or greed in others.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_theft: Sleight of hand, lockpicking and picking pockets.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_warfare: Tactics, command and knowledge of the battlefield.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_weaponry: Melee combat with swords, knives and hand weapons.
SVNSEA2E.DwEffect1: Death Spiral: +1 Bonus Die on your Risks.
SVNSEA2E.DwEffect2: Death Spiral: Villains roll 2 extra dice against you.
SVNSEA2E.DwEffect4: Death Spiral: Helpless — you can no longer act.
SVNSEA2E.DwTip1: 1 Dramatic Wound — you gain a +1 Bonus Die on every Risk you make.
SVNSEA2E.DwTip2: 2 Dramatic Wounds — Villains gain 2 Bonus Dice on Risks made against you.
SVNSEA2E.DwTip3: 3 Dramatic Wounds — your dice that land on 10 explode (roll again and add).
SVNSEA2E.DwTip4: 4 Dramatic Wounds — you are Helpless: you can no longer act until you heal.
SVNSEA2E.CorruptionInfo: Corruption comes from Evil Acts (1st = +1, 2nd = +2, 3rd = +3, 4th = +4, up to 10). Each time you gain Corruption the GM rolls 1d10 — if it's ≤ your Corruption, your Hero falls from grace and becomes a Villain. A 5-step Redemption Story removes 1 Corruption. (Core p.203)
SVNSEA2E.EvilActHint: GM only. The Hero commits an Evil Act: they gain escalating Corruption (1st +1, 2nd +2, 3rd +3, 4th +4), then the GM rolls 1d10 — a result of the Hero's Corruption or less means they Fall from Grace and become a Villain. (Core p.203)
SVNSEA2E.EvilActConfirm: {name} commits Evil Act #{act}, gaining {gain} Corruption (total {total} / 10). The GM will then roll 1d10 — a result of {total} or less turns the Hero into a Villain. Proceed?
SVNSEA2E.EvilActConfirmMaxed: {name} is already at maximum Corruption (10 / 10). Any further Evil Act is certain damnation — the GM rolls 1d10 and the Hero Falls from Grace. Proceed?
SVNSEA2E.FallFromGrace: {name} Falls from Grace — the Hero becomes a Villain!
SVNSEA2E.CorruptionRisk: Fall from Grace looms — a 1d10 of {n} or less would turn the Hero Villain.
SVNSEA2E.AdvStepsHint: Click a Step to mark it done as you achieve it in play.
SVNSEA2E.AdvRewardIntro: You don't spend experience — Heroes grow by finishing Stories. Choose the one Reward this Story earns; its cost sets how many Steps the Story takes.
SVNSEA2E.AdvConfigureIntro: Pick exactly what this Story earns.
SVNSEA2E.AdvStoryIntro: Name your Story and write its Goal — the specific deed that ends it — plus the first Step on the road there.
SVNSEA2E.AdvNewQuirkPlaceholder: e.g. Earn a Hero Point when your pride puts you in danger.
SVNSEA2E.AdvCorruptionIntro: Redemption is never easy or quick. This five-Step Story sheds a single point of Corruption.
SVNSEA2E.AdvStoryNamePlaceholder: A short name or mantra for this Story
SVNSEA2E.AdvGoalPlaceholder: The concrete deed that completes this Story.
SVNSEA2E.AdvFirstStepPlaceholder: The first action that puts you on the road to your Goal.
SVNSEA2E.AdvReviewFoot: Start this Story, then tick off its Steps on your sheet. Claim the Reward when the last Step is done.
SVNSEA2E.AdvH_skillRaise: Improve one Skill by one Rank (up to 5).
SVNSEA2E.AdvH_traitIncrease: Raise one Trait by one Rank. Only twice, ever.
SVNSEA2E.AdvH_traitShift: Move one Rank from one Trait to another.
SVNSEA2E.AdvH_advantage: Buy an Advantage from the compendium (also how you gain Sorcery).
SVNSEA2E.AdvH_arcanaChange: Swap your Virtue or Hubris for another.
SVNSEA2E.AdvH_quirkChange: Replace one of your Quirks with a new one.
SVNSEA2E.AdvH_corruptionRemove: Shed a single point of Corruption.
SVNSEA2E.AdvNoSkillRoom: All your Skills are already at Rank 5.
SVNSEA2E.AdvNoTraitRoom: No room to raise or shift Traits (max Rank 5, 15 total, two increases).
SVNSEA2E.AdvNoCompendia: The core compendia haven't loaded yet.
SVNSEA2E.AdvNoBackgrounds: You have no Backgrounds with a Quirk to change.
SVNSEA2E.AdvNoCorruption: You have no Corruption to remove.
SVNSEA2E.AdvNeedReward: Choose a Reward for this Story.
SVNSEA2E.AdvRewardUnavailable: That Reward isn't available right now.
SVNSEA2E.AdvSkillMaxed: That Skill is already at Rank 5.
SVNSEA2E.AdvTraitMaxed: That Trait is already at Rank 5.
SVNSEA2E.AdvTraitMinned: That Trait is already at its minimum (Rank 2).
SVNSEA2E.AdvTraitTotalCap: You can have at most 15 total Ranks of Traits.
SVNSEA2E.AdvTraitTwice: You may only increase a Trait twice, ever.
SVNSEA2E.AdvPickBothTraits: Pick a Trait to raise and a Trait to lower.
SVNSEA2E.AdvDistinctTraits: The two Traits must be different.
SVNSEA2E.AdvPickBackground: Pick which Background's Quirk to change.
SVNSEA2E.AdvAlreadyClaimed: This Story's Reward has already been claimed.
SVNSEA2E.AdvNotComplete: Finish every Step before claiming the Reward.
SVNSEA2E.AdvSourceMissing: Couldn't find that item in the compendium anymore.
